To determine the cluster centers, we calculate the mean of the x and y coordinates for each cluster. For C1 with points (0,6) and (6,0), the center is ((0+6)/2, (6+0)/2) which equals (3,3). For C2 containing (2,2), (4,4), and (6,6), the center is ((2+4+6)/3, (2+4+6)/3) resulting in (4,4). For C3 with points (5,5) and (7,7), the center is ((5+7)/2, (5+7)/2) yielding (6,6). Comparing these calculated centers to the options, Option A matches perfectly with C1:(3,3), C2:(4,4), and C3:(6,6).
******
The correct cluster centers are derived by averaging the coordinates within each group. C1 averages to (3,3), C2 averages to (4,4), and C3 averages to (6,6). This specific set of coordinates corresponds exactly to the values listed in Option A, confirming it as the accurate solution for the iteration described.
